name: Mixed sources with deep alias chains — tests multi-source discovery and alias resolution

input.userMessage: Extract the design tokens from this file

expectedBehaviors: Discovers BOTH local variables (Semantic, Scale collections) and library variables (Primitives from Acme)
Resolves the 3-level alias chain: color/text/primary -> Scale/neutral-900 -> Primitives/gray-900 -> #111827
Includes both the alias path AND the resolved literal value in the output
Differentiates local vs library sources in the output (e.g., source field or separate sections)
Captures typography styles alongside variable-based tokens
Resolves spacing/section -> Scale/space-800 -> 32 (2-level alias to literal)
Does not duplicate tokens that appear in both alias chain and source collection
antiPatterns: Only captures local variables and misses the Primitives library entirely
Stops alias resolution at the first level (reports color/text/primary as 'alias -> Scale/neutral-900' without resolving)
Resolves aliases but loses the alias path — only shows the final value with no traceability
Treats library variables as local or vice versa — no source differentiation
Misses typography styles because they are styles not variables
Produces duplicate entries for the same token at different alias levels
Crashes when an alias points to a library variable (cross-collection resolution)

evaluationCriteria.sourceDiscovery: Does it find tokens from both local collections AND the attached Acme library?
evaluationCriteria.aliasResolution: Does it resolve the full 3-level chain to the literal #111827 value?
evaluationCriteria.traceability: Does the output show the alias path so users can trace semantic -> scale -> primitive?
evaluationCriteria.sourceDifferentiation: Can you tell which tokens are local vs from the library?
evaluationCriteria.completeness: Are variables AND styles both captured in the final tokens.json?
evaluationCriteria.deduplication: Are tokens that appear at multiple alias levels represented once with proper references?
